# r4 forge contract repair

status: PARTIAL
delegation_outcome: completed

## Binding and authority

- Node binding: `r4-forge-contract-repair 9667df96675c`.
- Upstream authority read in full before implementation: `r3-lineage-proof-repair ef637b74db4b`.
- The frozen epoch-2 plan, r4 brief, and `r1-repair-blueprint e5345fa3e0ad` were read before editing.
- The implementation delegates committed recursive validation to r3's exported `verifyAllEpochSnapshots`; it does not construct or hash a second snapshot-authority projection.
- The schema-2 pre-commit authoring grammar still accepts `parent_snapshot_manifest_digest: pending`. Only a committed transaction is required to bind the exact projection digest.
- A committed schema-1 child may retain `pending` only when the same r3 recursive verifier proves the historical `legacy_external_binding` external-seal path.

## Exact initial failures

Command:

`node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-gitlab-contracts.js --forbidden-only plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/kaola-gitlab-workflow-plan-validator.js`

Initial result: exit 1.

Error excerpt: `kaola-gitlab-workflow-plan-validator.js contains forbidden reference: /\bgh\b/`.

Command:

`node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-gitea-contracts.js --forbidden-only plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/kaola-gitea-workflow-plan-validator.js`

Initial result: exit 1.

Error excerpt: `kaola-gitea-workflow-plan-validator.js contains forbidden reference: /\bglab\b/`.

## Root cause

1. The canonical operator-hint registry comment literally listed vendor CLI command names. Edition generation preserved those literals, so the unchanged GitLab and Gitea forbidden-token scanners correctly rejected the generated validators.
2. `validateCommittedEpochBinding` compared transaction and child scalars at finalization but did not bind `parent_snapshot_manifest_digest`, Case-B citations, or recursively verify the committed snapshot/child seals through r3's single projection authority. The pure authoring grammar correctly allowed `pending`, but committed validation failed to narrow that tolerance.
3. The validator CLI must load r3's verifier lazily to avoid a top-level cycle. During CLI execution, replan imports the still-loading validator, so the validator export table must be installed before `main()` invokes the lazy verifier.

## Files changed

- `scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` — canonical repair: forge-neutral comment; committed schema-2 projection/Case-B binding; schema-1 externally sealed compatibility; r3 recursive verifier delegation; export-before-main load ordering.
- `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js` — regenerated byte-identical Codex mirror from the full canonical file.
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/kaola-gitlab-workflow-plan-validator.js` — regenerated GitLab port from the full accumulated canonical diff.
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/kaola-gitea-workflow-plan-validator.js` — regenerated Gitea port from the full accumulated canonical diff.

No product/test/documentation file outside r4's declared four-file write set was edited by this node. The seeded evidence file is the role-authorized cache exception.

## Implementation result

- Canonical comments now refer only to `the forge CLI`; neither forbidden scanner was weakened.
- A schema-2 committed child must match `transaction.snapshot.authority_digest`; `pending`, arbitrary hex, and cross-transaction substitutions are rejected as `epoch_state_mismatch` before recursive verification.
- A diagnosis-to-build commit must match the transaction's exact `diagnosis_source_digest` and `recommended_shape_digest`; non-Case-B commits must not carry those citations.
- After scalar binding, finalization calls r3's `verifyAllEpochSnapshots(projectDir)`, which recursively enforces the projection, full manifest seal, child/attestation/transaction identity, descendant state, and generic externally sealed schema-1 compatibility.
- Plain `validatePlan` / `validateEpochContract` remains a pre-commit authoring gate and continues to accept the legitimate `pending` seed state.
- The canonical root was edited first with `apply_patch`; `node scripts/edition-sync.js --write` then regenerated all three owned mirrors from the full accumulated root file. It reported exactly 3 generated/mirrored files updated.

## Validation results

- `node scripts/test-replan.js` -> PASS: `test-replan: PASSED (888 assertions)`.
- `node --check` over all four plan-validator files -> PASS.
- In-memory schema-2 `pending` authoring probe through `validateEpochContract` -> PASS: `schema-2 pre-commit pending authoring state accepted`.
- `node scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js kaola-workflow/issue-699/workflow-plan.md --resume-check --json` -> PASS with plan hash `356e9948105a500db2dc3061b9fe3dc7c8dcdcf9e117df5c2c7eb23906d1f938`.
- Live externally sealed compatibility probe: `node scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js kaola-workflow/issue-699/workflow-plan.md --finalize-check --json` reached the later expected `chains_unverified` gate, proving committed epoch/snapshot validation passed without an internal/cycle/binding refusal.
- GitLab standalone forbidden-only command above -> PASS: `Kaola-Workflow GitLab forbidden-only check passed (1 file(s))`.
- Gitea standalone forbidden-only command above -> PASS: `Kaola-Workflow Gitea forbidden-only check passed (1 file(s))`.
- `node scripts/edition-sync.js --check` -> PASS: `12 forge aggregator ports, 25 COMMON_SCRIPTS mirrors, and 27 byte-identical groups in parity with canonical`.
- `node scripts/validate-script-sync.js` -> PASS: `25 common scripts, 27 byte-identical groups, 8 rename-normalized families, 2 hooks.json families, and 7 forge export-superset families in sync`.
- `git diff --check -- <the four owned validator files>` -> PASS.
- Final repository-wide `git diff --check` -> PASS.
- Owned diff-name check lists exactly the four declared validator files.

## Typed out-of-scope blocker

blocker_type: upstream_write_set_required
blocked_command_gitlab: `node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js`
blocked_command_gitea: `node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js`
blocked_disposition: no-loop; do not repair from r4

GitLab result: exit 1 at `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js:494`, `testWatchMrAbandonedClosureInvariantsClean`: `closure_invariants.ok must be true for abandoned MR`, with only violation `epoch-lineage-preserved`.

Gitea result: exit 1 at `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js:485`, `testWatchPrAbandonedClosureInvariantsClean`: `closure_invariants.ok must be true for abandoned PR`, with only violation `epoch-lineage-preserved`.

The symmetric root cause is outside r4: the MR/PR watch closed/abandoned branches build a closure receipt without setting `epoch_lineage_preserved`, while `emptyReceipt()` defaults that field to `failed`; `checkClosureInvariants` therefore reports a false lineage violation even though `archiveProjectDir` already ran the recursive snapshot verifier successfully. The likely repair belongs to the r2 claim/closure family and its packaged test fixtures, including canonical/Codex/GitLab/Gitea claim ports. Those paths are not in r4's exact four-file write set, so this node made no out-of-scope edit.

## Outcome

PARTIAL. The r4-owned validator/build repair is complete and all direct/focused owned checks pass. The two required packaged edition suites remain red only on the recorded upstream closure-receipt integration defect, which requires a separately authorized repair owner.
